root_text.sn56.31:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati sīsapāvane.
root_text.sn56.31:1.2: Atha kho bhagavā parittāni sīsapāpaṇṇāni pāṇinā gahetvā bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn56.31:1.5: yāni vā mayā parittāni sīsapāpaṇṇāni pāṇinā gahitāni yadidaṁ upari sīsapāvane”ti?
root_text.sn56.31:1.6: “Appamattakāni, bhante, bhagavatā parittāni sīsapāpaṇṇāni pāṇinā gahitāni;
root_text.sn56.31:1.7: atha kho etāneva bahutarāni yadidaṁ upari sīsapāvane”ti.
root_text.sn56.31:1.8: “Evameva kho, bhikkhave, etadeva bahutaraṁ yaṁ vo mayā abhiññāya anakkhātaṁ.
root_text.sn56.31:1.10: Na hetaṁ, bhikkhave, atthasaṁhitaṁ nādibrahmacariyakaṁ na nibbidāya na virāgāya na nirodhāya na upasamāya na abhiññāya na sambodhāya na nibbānāya saṁvattati;
root_text.sn56.31:2.2: ‘Idaṁ dukkhan’ti, bhikkhave, mayā akkhātaṁ, ‘ayaṁ dukkhasamudayo’ti mayā akkhātaṁ, ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodho’ti mayā akkhātaṁ, ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti mayā akkhātaṁ.
root_text.sn56.31:3.2: Etañhi, bhikkhave, atthasaṁhitaṁ etaṁ ādibrahmacariyakaṁ etaṁ nibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya upasamāya abhiññāya sambodhāya nibbānāya saṁvattati;
root_text.sn56.31:4.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.31:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Kosambī in a rosewood forest.
translation_text.sn56.31:1.2: Then the Buddha picked up a few rosewood leaves in his hand and addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn56.31:1.5: the few leaves in my hand, or those in the forest above me?”
translation_text.sn56.31:1.6: “Sir, the few leaves in your hand are a tiny amount.
translation_text.sn56.31:1.7: There are far more leaves in the forest above.”
translation_text.sn56.31:1.8: “In the same way, there is much more that I have directly known but have not explained to you. What I have explained is a tiny amount.
translation_text.sn56.31:1.9: And why haven’t I explained it?
translation_text.sn56.31:1.10: Because it’s not beneficial or relevant to the fundamentals of the spiritual life. It doesn’t lead to disillusionment, dispassion, cessation, peace, insight, awakening, and extinguishment.
translation_text.sn56.31:1.11: That’s why I haven’t explained it.
translation_text.sn56.31:2.2: I have explained: ‘This is suffering’ … ‘This is the origin of suffering’ … ‘This is the cessation of suffering’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering’.
translation_text.sn56.31:3.1: And why have I explained this?
translation_text.sn56.31:3.2: Because it’s beneficial and relevant to the fundamentals of the spiritual life. It leads to disillusionment, dispassion, cessation, peace, insight, awakening, and extinguishment.
translation_text.sn56.31:4.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”
